task: I’m helping someone look into treatment options for lung cancer in the U.S., and I want a solid browser-based shortlist I can actually review myself afterward. Please go to ClinicalTrials.gov and search for interventional lung cancer studies in the United States that are currently recruiting, then use the site filters so we’re only looking at active recruiting trials that are really relevant. As you find good candidates, open the official ClinicalTrials.gov record for each one in its own tab and keep those tabs open so I can compare them later. I need at least 15 distinct trials, and for each one please verify on the actual trial page that it’s recruiting, note the study phase, identify the treatment or intervention type, and capture the U.S. locations where it’s available. Once you’ve gathered the set, create a CryptPad Documents file titled “Lung Cancer Clinical Trials” and record one entry per trial with the trial name, treatment type, trial phase, recruiting status, locations, and the official ClinicalTrials.gov link. After that, add a short summary telling me which treatment approaches seem to come up most often and which cities or hospitals show up most frequently across the location lists. Before you finish, do one last pass to make sure the document has at least 15 complete entries and that each entry still matches an open official trial tab so I have visual proof to review.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully searched ClinicalTrials.gov using the required filters (Lung Cancer, Recruiting, Interventional, United States) as evidenced by the URL parameters in the action history and the resulting list of trials.

R2: success - The agent opened 15 distinct official ClinicalTrials.gov study records in separate tabs, as confirmed by the action history and the final verification step.

R3: success - The agent captured the trial names and recruiting status for the 15 selected trials, and the document entries match the official records.

R4: success - The agent included the study phase and treatment/intervention type for each trial, which were extracted from the official records.

R5: success - The agent included U.S. recruiting locations for each trial, as verified by the content of the CryptPad document.

R6: success - The agent validated the final set of studies to ensure they were lung cancer-related, interventional, recruiting, and U.S.-based, and confirmed the completeness of the entries.

R7: success - The agent created a CryptPad document titled 'Lung Cancer Clinical Trials' containing 15 complete entries with all required fields and links, as shown in the final screenshots.

R8: success - The document includes a summary section detailing common treatment approaches and frequent locations, and the official tabs remained available for cross-checking.

rubric_results.requirement: ClinicalTrials.gov is searched with filters that limit results to U.S.-based interventional lung cancer studies that are currently recruiting.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can see the ClinicalTrials.gov results page with relevant search terms and visible recruiting/interventional/U.S. filtering applied.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully searched ClinicalTrials.gov using the required filters (Lung Cancer, Recruiting, Interventional, United States) as evidenced by the URL parameters in the action history and the resulting list of trials.

rubric_results.requirement: At least 15 distinct official ClinicalTrials.gov study records are opened in separate tabs and left open.
rubric_results.verification: Browser shows 15 or more open tabs corresponding to individual ClinicalTrials.gov trial pages, not just search results.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent opened 15 distinct official ClinicalTrials.gov study records in separate tabs, as confirmed by the action history and the final verification step.

rubric_results.requirement: Each selected trial has its official trial name and recruiting status captured from the official study page, with recruiting status verified as active recruiting.
rubric_results.verification: Document entries match visible trial titles and recruiting status on the open ClinicalTrials.gov tabs.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent captured the trial names and recruiting status for the 15 selected trials, and the document entries match the official records.

rubric_results.requirement: Each selected trial includes the study phase and treatment or intervention type taken from the official record.
rubric_results.verification: For sampled entries, the phase and intervention details in the document match the corresponding fields on the open ClinicalTrials.gov pages.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent included the study phase and treatment/intervention type for each trial, which were extracted from the official records.

rubric_results.requirement: Each selected trial includes U.S. recruiting locations from the official record.
rubric_results.verification: For sampled entries, the listed cities or hospitals in the document match the locations section on the corresponding ClinicalTrials.gov pages.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent included U.S. recruiting locations for each trial, as verified by the content of the CryptPad document.

rubric_results.requirement: The final set is validated so every included study is lung cancer related, interventional, currently recruiting, U.S.-based, and complete for all required fields, with any invalid studies replaced.
rubric_results.verification: Final document contains only qualifying studies, and any replacements correspond to open official tabs that satisfy the criteria.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent validated the final set of studies to ensure they were lung cancer-related, interventional, recruiting, and U.S.-based, and confirmed the completeness of the entries.

rubric_results.requirement: A CryptPad Document titled 'Lung Cancer Clinical Trials' is created and contains at least 15 entries with trial name, treatment type, trial phase, recruiting status, locations, and official ClinicalTrials.gov link.
rubric_results.verification: CryptPad Document title is visible and the body contains 15 or more complete entries with all required fields and links.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent created a CryptPad document titled 'Lung Cancer Clinical Trials' containing 15 complete entries with all required fields and links, as shown in the final screenshots.

rubric_results.requirement: The document includes a summary of the most common treatment approaches and the most frequent cities or hospitals, and the open official tabs remain available for visual cross-checking.
rubric_results.verification: A summary section is visible in the CryptPad Document, and the browser still shows the official ClinicalTrials.gov tabs open.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The document includes a summary section detailing common treatment approaches and frequent locations, and the official tabs remained available for cross-checking.
